coldStandardsFound.title: § 1926.650 Scope, application, and definitions applicable to this subpart.

evidenceNeededDraft: Identify the specific applicable equipment/systems or workplace location involved.
Confirm whether physical or administrative safety controls for caught_in_between are in place and operational.
Review training records, inspection checklists, or maintenance documentation relevant to excavation_trenching.
Verify conformance with standard citations: 29 CFR 1926.650, 29 CFR 1926.651, 29 CFR 1926.652.
[Advisory Only] Qualified safety personnel must verify specific evidence requirements on-site.
correctiveActionPatternsDraft: Restrict access to or isolate any hazardous conditions related to caught_in_between immediately.
Inspect, repair, or replace any defective components of the affected equipment before returning to service.
Implement engineering, administrative, or PPE controls to minimize risks from caught_in_between.
Instruct workers on safe practices regarding excavation_trenching and verify procedural compliance.
Document corrective actions completed and ensure compliance with citations: 29 CFR 1926.650, 29 CFR 1926.651, 29 CFR 1926.652.
[Advisory Only] Corrective action details must be reviewed and approved by qualified safety managers or engineers.
applicabilityDraft: Applies to operations within jurisdiction: osha_construction.
Applies when task, activity, or condition involves equipment/tools or associated subsystems.
Applies when workers are exposed to caught_in_between risks during normal or maintenance operations.
[Advisory Only] Scope of applicability must be validated by a qualified inspector or safety professional.
